rulings.Case Title: ANA MACDONALD VS. DUKE PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 16, 2017 line 3. DEFENDANT DUKE PARTNERS, LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a cause of action providing for substantive basis of liability challenging the foreclosure. See Distor v. U.S. Bank (2009) 2009 WL 3429700; Lopez v. Chase Home Financial (2009) 2009 WL 981676; Glue-Ford, Inc. v. Slautterback Corp. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1018. Cancellation of Deed and Quiet Title are not proper means to "challenge the foreclosure." All other causes of action in the operative complaint seek damages and prospective injunction, no cause of action seeks to "undo the foreclosure." Additionally, if Plaintiffs continue to assert a cause of action for quiet title in the amended complaint Plaintiffs shall allege facts to support Macdonald's standing in light of the transfer of title in 2004 and to allege repayment of debt. The Court does not address Defendant's BFP argument at this time.= (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ISA MUHAWIEH VS. ENEYDA CHUN GOMEZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 16, 2017 line 4. DEFENDANT ENEYDA GOMEZ and JOAQUIN MARTINEZ's Motion To Continue Trial DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CCP 1170.5(b) and (c) requirements. CCP 1170.5 is not mentioned in the papers and no hearing per CCP 1170.5(c) is requested; no relevant evidence is presented. The Court also notes that Defendant failed to comply with CRC 3.1300(c).= (501/AJR)
